title: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan whose reaction to trade went viral
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown met with six-year-old fan Giovanni Jean after his tearful reaction to Brown's trade went viral.
Brown, now with the 76ers, connected with Jean at his summer bridge program at M.I.T.
Giovanni's mother praised the meeting, noting her son's admiration for Brown and his love for education and sports.

title: Fatal Cambridge shooting renews criticism of council vote to end ShotSpotter contract
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Xavier Bautista was shot and killed in Cambridge, with his body found an hour later near Broadway and Norfolk Street.
Cambridge's police unions blame the City Council's removal of ShotSpotter for delayed emergency response to the shooting.
Bautista's family seeks justice; a GoFundMe has raised nearly $14,000 to support them after his death.

title: Jaylen Brown on Celtics trade: Boston is home and no organization can take that away
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown expresses continued love for Boston despite being traded from the Celtics to the 76ers.
Brown's 7uice Foundation summer program at M.I.T. in Cambridge continues his community efforts in Boston.
Massachusetts Governor declared July 7th as 7uice Foundation Day, honoring Brown's contributions beyond basketball.

title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, from Lawrence, faces drunk driving charges after crashing a Porsche on I-95 in Woburn.
Tarshi was traveling northbound on I-95 south and crashed by Exit 54A; no injuries were reported.
A forged New Hampshire RMV document was found in the vehicle, and further charges may be pending.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in 'The Five-Star Weekend' debuting on Peacock July 9, filmed on Nantucket.
HBO Max celebrates Shark Week with additional content, kicking off on July 26, including horror classic 'Jaws' on Peacock.
Boston-filmed A24 movie 'The Drama' premieres on HBO Max on July 31; other highlights include 'Project Hail Mary' on Prime.

title: Noah Kahan’s historic 4-show Fenway Park run kicks off Tuesday. Here’s everything you need to know.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan begins his four-show run at Fenway Park in Boston, part of 'The Great Divide' Tour.
Shows are scheduled for Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, and Saturday, with gates opening at 5 p.m.
Each night will include fireworks if weather permits, confirmed by the Boston Fire Department.

title: Labouré College cuts 65 jobs ahead of Curry College transition
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Labouré College is cutting 65 jobs as it closes its Milton campus and transitions to Curry College.
Curry College will incorporate Labouré programs and launch the Labouré Center for Advancing Healthcare Opportunity.
Labouré faced financial and enrollment challenges, with around 530 students and 94 faculty members in 2024.

title: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr. to multi-year contract
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr., 26, to a multi-year deal worth up to $13.7 million over four years.
Harper Jr. averaged 4.2 points and 1.7 rebounds across 29 games for the Celtics last season.
He earned third-team All-NBA G-League honors, averaging 25.4 points in 18 games with the Maine Celtics.

title: ‘Amateur hour’: Mass. sheriff accuses ICE of trying to bully him into breaking the law
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Bristol County Sheriff Paul Heroux accuses ICE of intimidation for requesting detainees be held despite state law.
ICE detained Jose Raul Martinez-Alvarado with charges including unlawful firearm possession after his release on bail.
Massachusetts law prohibits enforcing federal immigration detainers without a 287(g) agreement; Heroux cites legal precedence.

title: The murder of Karina Holmer remains unsolved 30 years later; police asking public for help
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Karina Holmer, a 20-year-old au pair from Sweden, was murdered in Boston on June 21, 1996; her case remains unsolved.
Holmer's dismembered body was found near Boylston Street, but the crime scene was never located and the lower half was missing.
Boston Police seek public assistance for new information in the investigation, which remains active 30 years later.
